programme.title: Buddy Holly
programme.desc: With his signature vocal hiccup and hits like "That'll Be the Day," "Rave On," "Peggy Sue" and "Not Fade Away," Buddy Holly was a rock & roll pioneer, as well as one of the genre's first great singer-songwriters. He used the recording studio for doubletracking and other advanced techniques, and popularized the two guitars, bass, and drums lineup. Holly's playful, mock-ingenuous singing, with slides between falsetto and regular voice, was a major influence on Paul McCartney, Bob Dylan, and numerous imitators. When he died in an airplane crash at 22, he had been recording for less than two years.

programme.title: Chuck Berry
programme.desc: Chuck Berry was one of the most influential rock 'n' roll performers in music history. He's known for songs including "Maybellene" and "Johnny B. Goode.

programme.title: Various Artists
programme.desc: The best of Qello Concerts

programme.title: Marvin Gaye
programme.desc: Marvin Gaye- Born in 1939, in Washington, D.C., Marvin Gaye sang in his father's church and in the Moonglows before signing with Motown. He recorded songs by Smokey Robinson before becoming his own producer on the protest album What's Going On (1971). Gaye was killed in 1984 during a domestic dispute with his father.

programme.title: Al Green
programme.desc: Al Green is known for the hit song "Let's Stay Together," and for leaving his musical career at its height in the 1970s to become a reverend at his own church.

programme.title: Various Artists
programme.desc: The best of Qello Concerts

programme.title: The Mamas and Papas
programme.desc: The Mamas and Papas were a four-piece folk act whose high harmonies and blend of easygoing, trippy originals with nicely stylized covers virtually soundtracked the late '60s. Leading music critics cast fresh light on the career of The Mamas and Papas.

programme.title: Crosby Stills Nash & Young
programme.desc: Rock's first supergroup, Crosby, Stills, Nash, and Young each had successful recording careers before coming together—Crosby with the Byrds, Stills and Young with Buffalo Springfield, and Nash with the Hollies—and each has been inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame at least twice. Leading music critics cast fresh light on the career of Crosby Stills Nash & Young.

programme.title: Various Artists
programme.desc: The best of Qello Concerts

programme.title: Lynyrd Skynyrd
programme.desc: The quintessential Southern rock band, Lynyrd Skynyrd rose to prominence in 1973, full of regional pride and stressing cocky, boisterous hard rock as opposed to the Allman Brothers' more open-ended blues. Their signature song, "Freebird," complete with it fiery five-minute, three-guitar solo, is easily the most requested live song in existence. When the band broke up in 1977, after Ronnie Van Zant, Steve Gaines and Cassie Gaines died in a plane crash, rock suffered a tremendous loss.

programme.title: Johnny Cash
programme.desc: A documentary that sweeps you along with the flow of musical genius that is Johnny Cash. From his family's poverty in the Great Depression to touring with Elvis and becoming a legend in his own right. We see his reinvention for a whole new generation with Rick Rubin and how ‘Hurt' became the epitaph to his amazing life and career.

programme.title: Various Artists
programme.desc: The best of Qello Concerts

programme.title: Black Sabbath
programme.desc: Birmingham in the late 1960s was an industrial city, packed with music groups. At its height, the New Musical Express had reported 500 on its bustling circuit and from two of these groups, Black Sabbath was formed. The Beatles would be a key influence for Ozzy, but bassist Geezer Butler also shaped Ozzy's work, through his lyrics After signing to Vertigo Records in 1970, the four piece used their distinctive sound to full effect for their self-titled debut album, Black Sabbath. Black Sabbath became a commercial success, entering the UK top 10 and the US charts. In it's first year it sold one million copies. Keen to build on the success, the band entered the studio just four months after releasing their debut. The title track, Paranoid, would lead the new album. Featuring archive material, music videos “PARANOID” “EVIL WOMAN” and, exclusive insights from music critics and journalists JOHN AIZLEWOOD (Evening Standard), DANIELLE PERRY (XFM), WILL HODGKINSON (The Times) & CAMILLA PIA (BBC Radio 6)

programme.title: Flying Over Spanish Islands
programme.desc: In their first collaboration, cinematographer Stefan Zimmerman and Nature Relaxation™ producer David Huting present "Above the Spanish Islands." This 50-minute 4K aerial film captures Mallorca and the Canary Islands, set to ambient music by Diamans, Electus, and Michael St. Laurent.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland: Part I" provides a soothing aerial journey through eastern Iceland's fjords and valleys, starting at sunrise in Brei_dalsvik. Enjoy views of mists, a waterfall, and the Brei_dals' river, set to nature sounds and music by The Cynic Project, showcasing East Iceland's beauty.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland 2
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland II: Summer in the Golden Circle" presents a serene aerial view of Iceland's south coast, starting at Skogafoss and moving to Vik, with black sand beaches, cliffs, and Vatnajökull glacier. Filmed in 2020, this 1-hour film features local wildlife and immersive music.

programme.title: Flying Over Kauai
programme.desc: "Flying Over Kauai" provides a soothing aerial journey over Hawaii's Garden Island, featuring the lush NaPali Coast, waterfalls, canyons, and beaches. Paired with new music by The Cynic Project and nature sounds, this 4K film offers a visually and sonically immersive experience.

programme.title: Flying Over Oahu
programme.desc: "Flying Over Oahu" offers a 97-minute aerial journey over Oahu's iconic coastlines with nature sounds and music by Alex Smith (The Cynic Project). From Waikiki Beach to Turtle Bay, enjoy breathtaking 4K landscapes, ending with a stunning Waikiki sunset.

programme.title: ART Escape
programme.desc: Discover an enchanting collection featuring breathtaking photographs of epic waterscapes. Immerse yourself in the picturesque beauty of serene lakes, meandering rivers, and vast oceans, capturing the awe-inspiring harmony of water's natural grandeur.

programme.title: ART Buzz
programme.desc: A vivid and energizing collection showcasing the dynamic and graphic nature of street art and bold illustrations, celebrating the rebellious spirit of urban culture.

programme.title: ARTspiration
programme.desc: Step into the surreal world of Vanessa Stefanova, a Sydney-based artist blending visual metaphors with spontaneous digital creations. This collection captures her weekly rhythm of painting and digital art—each piece a vivid snapshot of thought, mood, and moment.

programme.title: ARTspiration
programme.desc: Lauren Karp's work blends abstract landscapes with layered images, capturing the earth's rhythm through color, pattern, and light. Her pieces evoke fleeting moments reimagined through movement and emotion.

programme.title: ARTspiration
programme.desc: Explore Andrew Soria's mesmerizing digital photo collages in “Cityscape Mementos.” His unique artistic vision offers a fresh perspective on urban architecture and culture, inviting viewers to rediscover their own cities in a new light.

programme.title: ARTspiration
programme.desc: Discover the cinematic world of Michelangelo Rossi, a self-taught Italian painter whose oil works evoke memory, light, and silence. His layered brushwork and timeless settings invite reflection, capturing quiet moments filled with emotion and depth.

programme.title: ART Buzz
programme.desc: A visual fiesta, radiating the energy of celebration and unity. From intricate depictions of traditional dancers to modern interpretations of iconic symbols, the art encapsulates Mexico's rich history while the music adds to the joyful atmosphere.

programme.title: ART Buzz
programme.desc: A transcendent journey celebrating the vibrancy & diversity of black culture. From evocative photographs to vivid canvases, this collection pays homage to the global tapestry of black identity, uniting voices and visions that resonate with the soul.
